decisions.title: Controller Design
decisions.summary: Controllers should be thin and focus on exposing HTTP endpoints. They should delegate business logic to services.

decisions.title: Full-Stack Auction Management System
decisions.summary: A Chinese Auction/Raffle Management System with Angular frontend and ASP.NET Core backend, featuring roles for Admin, Donor, and Users.

decisions.title: Tech Stack Decision
decisions.summary: Chosen tech stack: Angular, TypeScript, SCSS for frontend; .NET 8, ASP.NET Core Web API, EF Core with SQL Server, Serilog, Swashbuckle (Swagger) for backend.

decisions.title: Project Structure Decision
decisions.summary: Defined project structure with `/client` for Angular and `/server` for ASP.NET Core Web API, including key files and solution definitions.

decisions.title: Repository Design and Data Access
decisions.summary: Repositories are designed to handle data access and encapsulate Entity Framework Core operations. They should focus on queries and updates, keeping business logic separate.

decisions.title: Asynchronous API Usage
decisions.summary: Use async EF Core APIs like `ToListAsync` and `FirstOrDefaultAsync` for improved performance and responsiveness.

decisions.title: Data Mapping and DTOs
decisions.summary: Repositories should return domain models or EF entities. Mapping to DTOs should be done in the service layer to maintain a clear separation of concerns and simplify testing.

decisions.title: Performance Optimization
decisions.summary: For performance-critical scenarios, consider returning lightweight domain projections (value objects) from the repository and performing mapping in the service layer.

decisions.title: Migration Management
decisions.summary: When adding migrations, ensure you run EF Core commands from the `server/` directory and confirm the `DefaultConnection` setting is correct.

decisions.title: Service Design
decisions.summary: Services should contain business logic and orchestrate various components. They should be unit-testable, small, and single-responsibility.

decisions.title: Dependency Injection
decisions.summary: Use constructor injection for dependencies like repositories and external integrations to keep services testable.

decisions.title: Async/Await Usage
decisions.summary: Utilize async/await for I/O-bound operations and return Task-based signatures for better performance and scalability.

decisions.title: Exception Handling
decisions.summary: Handle exceptions at boundaries and let middleware produce consistent error responses for a unified error handling approach.

decisions.title: Data Mapping
decisions.summary: Services are responsible for mapping domain models/entities to DTOs. Use AutoMapper profiles or manual mapping, but avoid mapping in repositories.

rules.rule: Keep controllers thin and focused on HTTP interactions.
Utilize attribute routing and action attributes for HTTP methods.
Use model binding and DTOs for request and response handling.
Apply authorization attributes for role-based access control.
Implement validation using model validation attributes.
Use async actions and return Tasks for scalability.
Follow existing naming conventions: PascalCase for public types/methods, camelCase for private fields with _ prefix.
Keep controllers thin, business logic in Services/Repositories.
Use async/await for I/O-bound operations, return Task-based signatures.
Use dependency injection, register new services in `Program.cs`.
Add unit tests for new services/controllers in `SERVER.Tests`.
Use Angular CLI for generators, preserve existing TypeScript/SCSS styles and module structure.
Repository methods should focus on data access (queries, updates) and avoid business logic.
Use async EF Core APIs for improved performance.
Repositories MUST NOT perform mapping to DTOs; mapping should be handled by the service layer.
For performance-optimized data shapes, return lightweight domain projections from the repository and map in the service layer.
Keep services unit-testable by injecting dependencies via constructor injection.
Prefer small, single-responsibility services. Extract helper services if methods become large.
Use async/await for I/O-bound operations and return Task-based signatures.
Handle exceptions at boundaries and use middleware for consistent error responses.
Map domain models/entities to DTOs in services, not in repositories.

warnings.message: Do not accept user ID or role from client input.
warnings.mitigation: Extract identity claims from authenticated user and pass to service.

warnings.message: Do not run UNIX-specific shell commands. Use powershell-compatible commands for local instructions/execution.
warnings.mitigation: Ensure all shell commands are compatible with Windows PowerShell.

warnings.message: EF Core is configured for SQL Server. Ensure a reachable SQL Server instance and `DefaultConnection` in `appsettings.json`.
warnings.mitigation: Confirm SQL Server setup and connection details before running migrations or the server.

warnings.message: App uses user secrets. Avoid reading secrets from external stores in ephemeral environments.
warnings.mitigation: Mock secrets for local development or use environment variables for sensitive data.

warnings.message: Serilog writes to console and `server/Logs/log-.txt`. Avoid deleting logs blindly.
warnings.mitigation: Implement log rotation or trimming for old entries instead of deleting.
